Cross-section: long the top-decile imbalance names, short the bottom decile (dollar-neutral) to strip beta, OR long/flat single-name. Run through the standard harness.

CONTROLS (run all, the project's bar):
- vs Buy-and-Hold: must beat B&H net (every single-asset price-only family has failed this; MN strips it).
- beta-decomp: regress returns on SPY; require alpha after hedge (dollar-neutral construction handles most).
- OOS split + out-of-regime: but NOTE the deep history is DAILY only (10.5yr SIP) — top-of-book imbalance is an INTRADAY signal, so out-of-regime 2016-2026 cannot be run at the signal's native frequency; only the few cached quote-days exist. This is itself a near-fatal limitation: no multi-regime intraday quote history.
- COST SWEEP (THE decisive control): 0/1/2/5 bps per leg, exactly as Case 17. Top-of-book imbalance entries are high-turnover (multiple round-trips/day); Alpaca fills are ~1.2s with slippery opens, so 2bps is optimistic.
- DSR-deflate for number of trials (window/entry/exit grid).
- shuffle-placebo: re-run with quote-event order shuffled; real signal must beat placebo.
- survivorship: universe is liquid large-caps already in cache (selection by liquidity, not return) — low survivorship risk but note it.

TRACTABLE SUBSET: only the L1OFI top-of-book version on SPY/QQQ/AAPL over the handful of cached full-session quote-days. The literal top-3-depth strategy is not tractable at all.

novel_feasible.name: TD3 Continuous Trading Position Adjustment
novel_feasible.signal_rule: RL actor-critic (TD3) learns continuous action policy: Long (buy), Short (sell), Hold. Actions output position size (0.0-1.0 scale). Open position at SOD, close at EOD. Actor explores with noise; critic evaluates value function
data_needs: State space: price, returns, volatility, indicators
novel_feasible.predicted_kill: Beta-decomposition + vs-buy-and-hold on the out-of-universe / out-of-regime holdout. A TD3 actor mapping (price, returns, vol, indicators) -> continuous position size on a single asset's daily bar is a learned SIZING OVERLAY on close-to-close beta. Its only profitable policy on a long-biased equity is "stay long," which the harness scores as risk-reduced beta that NEVER beats buy-and-hold's total return (the Case-2 result that holds for every single-asset directional family). Any apparent alpha is the highest-capacity overfit the program has ever tested: with far more free parameters than the formulaic-alpha zoo (0/21 Bonferroni) or PCA stat-arb (IS 0.99 -> OOS -1.18), in-sample Sharpe will look great and then collapse on (a) the fresh-symbol holdout, (b) out-of-regime years (train pre-2022, the agent overfits the bull tape and dies in the 2022 bear), and (c) the shuffle-placebo (policy trained on shuffled returns scores ~the same). The daily flip turnover then eats whatever survives at ~2 bps/leg taker cost. Single most likely single kill: out-of-regime per-year Sharpe goes negative AND it fails vs-B&H — beta wearing a neural net.
novel_feasible.test_plan:
DATA (all already on-venue, nothing new to fetch): ~210 cached SIP daily-bar symbols at /Volumes/My Passport/AlpcaData/cache/*_1day_bars.jsonl, ~10.5yr (2016-2026). Use AAPL/SPY-style liquid names plus a disjoint fresh-symbol set for the holdout. State = [normalized price, lagged returns (1/5/21d), realized vol (21d), RSI/ADX/MACD as in existing strategies]; action = continuous position in [-1,1] (short..long); reward = next-day position-weighted return minus per-trade cost. Open-SOD/close-EOD reduces to acting on daily close-to-close (the only return the daily bar exposes); model spread+impact on the daily rebalance.

CONSTRUCTION: implement TD3 (actor + twin critics, target nets, delayed policy update, exploration noise) in numpy/torch as alpca/backtest/td3_sizing.py producing a daily position series -> equity curve, then feed that equity curve straight into alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (EvalReport already gives vs-B&H, beta/alpha, IR, t-stat/p, segment Sharpes, OOS split, DSR). Fix a-priori hyperparameters BEFORE seeing the test set; report mean +/- std over >=10 random seeds (RL variance is huge) -- a single lucky seed is the cardinal RL self-deception and must not be cherry-picked.

CONTROLS (must clear the documented 'validated' bar):
1. vs Buy-and-Hold: strat total return must exceed B&H total return, not just Sharpe (catches risk-reduced beta).
2. Beta-decomposition (beta_alpha): regress equity on SPY; require alpha>0 with beta near 0 -- if profit is beta*market, reject.
3. OOS / walk-forward: train agent on in-sample window, evaluate ONLY on held-out forward bars (evaluate(oos_frac=0.3)); expanding-window walk-forward retrain.
4. Out-of-regime: train pre-2022, test on the 2022 bear + 2023-2026 separately; per-year Sharpe must be positive in a majority of years (kills bull-tape overfit).
5. Out-of-universe / fresh-symbol holdout: select all hyperparameters on a train symbol set, report on a DISJOINT symbol set (the single most valuable control -- killed EAR-PEAD +0.68 -> -0.52).
6. Shuffle placebo: retrain the identical TD3 pipeline on shuffled/blockwise-permuted returns; if real Sharpe ~= shuffled Sharpe, the policy learned fitted noise.
7. Cost wall: charge realistic taker spread+impact (~2 bps/leg, escalate) on every daily position change; daily flips make this decisive.
8. DSR / Bonferroni: deflate Sharpe by the true trial count (seeds x hyperparam grid x architectures searched) -- RL's effective search breadth is large.
9. Survivorship: since the train/test universe is selection-based (today's liquid survivors), rerun on a point-in-time / delisted-inclusive name set to confirm the edge is not a survivor artifact.
10. Forward paper-track: if (and only if) it somehow clears 1-9, deploy ~1 share on Alpaca paper for independent live resolution.

PASS BAR (non-negotiable): beats B&H total return, positive net-of-cost alpha with low beta, OOS+walk-forward positive, positive in a majority of calendar years out-of-regime, beats its own shuffle placebo, survives DSR at honest trial count and the fresh-symbol holdout, and is not a survivorship artifact -- across seeds, not one lucky run.

novel_feasible.name: Pivot Bands Scalping Strategy
novel_feasible.signal_rule: Long if ADX < 25, candles close at bottom green band, Williams %R > MA; exit at top red band or %R below MA
novel_feasible.predicted_kill: COST WALL (turnover x spread/impact). This is a per-candle intraday scalp: long when an in-chop candle (ADX<25) closes on the bottom band with Williams %R above its MA, exit at the top band. That fires multiple round-trips per day. Alpca already has the canonical precedent: Case 17 (overnight->intraday reversal, docs/EDGE_CASE_STUDIES.md:41) was a control-CONFIRMED REAL anomaly (gross Sharpe 0.93, DSR 0.90) that collapsed to -0.41 at 2bps/leg purely from ~2x/day turnover -- and 2bps is optimistic given measured ~1.2s Alpaca fills with slippery prints. A band-scalp turns the book over far MORE than 2x/day, so its breakeven cost is well below 1bp/leg; it will be negative at any realistic IEX/SIP top-of-book cost. Whatever gross edge a single-band-touch pivot scalp shows on SPY/QQQ will not survive the spread, and there are no maker rebates / no L2 to improve fills on this venue.
novel_feasible.test_plan:
DATA (and its hard ceiling): The strategy is intraday by construction (candles closing on bands, %R vs MA, exit at opposite band => minute/5-min grain). Alpca's deep 10.5yr/195-symbol history is DAILY bars only -- useless for a scalp. Verified intraday store = ONLY 3 symbols with 1-min bars: /Volumes/My Passport/AlpcaData/cache/{SPY,QQQ,AAPL}_1min_bars.jsonl, ~291k bars each, spanning 750 trading days 2023-06-02 to 2026-06-01 (~3yr). So every test below is confined to SPY/QQQ/AAPL on 2023-2026 minute bars (resample to 1-min and 5-min candles; regular-hours only).

CONSTRUCTION: New module alpca/backtest/pivot_bands.py. Pivot bands = classic floor-trader pivot from prior session's H/L/C (P, R1/R2/R3, S1/S2/S3) recomputed each session via on_session_start (pattern already in alpca/strategies/intraday.py); "bottom green band" = price touching S1/S2, "top red band" = R1/R2. ADX(14) via existing compute_adx (alpca/strategies/momentum.py:367). Williams %R(14) + its SMA computed in-module. Entry: ADX<25 AND candle closes at/below lower band AND %R > %R-MA -> long; exit at upper band OR %R < %R-MA. Add the symmetric short. NO LOOKAHEAD: pivots from PRIOR session, all indicators causal, entry on candle CLOSE filled at next bar open. Per-leg cost applied on every fill.

CONTROLS (the full gauntlet, mirroring audit_overfit.py + Case 17):
1. vs Buy&Hold: net equity must beat B&H of the same symbol over 2023-2026 (per docs/BASELINE.md the standing bar -- single-asset price strategies have all been beta, not alpha).
2. Beta-decomp: beta_alpha() (evaluation.py:160) vs SPY -- a long-biased scalp on SPY/QQQ is almost pure beta; require alpha t-stat>0 after hedging.
3. COST SWEEP (the decisive test): sharpe at 0 / 0.5 / 1 / 2 / 5 bps per leg. Report turnover/day and the breakeven cost. Realistic floor = >=2bps given ~1.2s Alpaca fills (REALISM.md). This is the gate the strategy will fail.
4. OOS split: fit any params (band choice, ADX thr, %R window) on 2023-2024, test untouched 2025-2026.
5. Out-of-regime: ATTEMPTED but BLOCKED -- no intraday before 2023, so no 2016-2022 / no vol-spike regime. Flag explicitly: cannot clear the out-of-regime bar that the memory says is now REQUIRED for "validated".
6. DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio() at honest trial count (every band/ADX/%R combo swept + the project's prior search breadth, n>=100) -- net DSR must stay >0.9.
7. Survivorship/breadth: only 3 highly-correlated mega-liquid names; cannot demonstrate out-of-universe generalization (the exact test that killed EAR-PEAD Case 18). Treat 3-symbol agreement as one effective bet, not breadth.
8. Shuffle placebo + momentum/mean-reversion sign control (Case 17/19 pattern): a real band-reversion edge must beat a returns-shuffled placebo and flip sign vs a same-turnover momentum control.

DECISION RULE: ship ONLY if net Sharpe (>=2bps) > B&H, alpha t-stat>0, net DSR>0.9 OOS, beats shuffle placebo. Predicted: passes gross, dies at <=1bp like Case 17 -> REJECT, archive as another cost-wall case. Run: .venv/bin/python scripts/backtest_cached.py-style driver loading the 3 intraday files.

novel_feasible.name: LSTM Neural Network Price Prediction
novel_feasible.signal_rule: Employ LSTM networks trained on historical price data; generate buy/sell signals based on next-period price predictions (if predicted_price > current_price, buy; else sell)
novel_feasible.predicted_kill: Beta + fresh-symbol holdout. The "if predicted_price > current then buy" rule on a single liquid asset is, mechanically, a learned long-biased trend-follower. In a 10.5yr bull tape it will post a positive return and a decent Sharpe, but it will NOT beat buy-and-hold on return (it under-exposes), so beta_alpha will show beta~0.6-0.9 and alpha~0 — exactly Case 2 (rsi-mr/ema-momentum/supertrend all "GENUINE-looking" yet all just risk-reduced beta, none beat B&H OOS). The cross-sectional/L-S variant dies one rung later on the fresh-symbol + out-of-regime holdout, the same gate that has now killed ~7 candidates (factor zoo, Alpha101/158/GTJA191 zoo Case 56 best-DSR 0.596 with OOS winners INVERTING out-of-universe, EAR-PEAD x3). An LSTM with 50 units / 100 epochs / free feature engineering has a vastly larger overfitting surface than any of those, so train-set Sharpe will be high and disjoint-symbol/held-out-year Sharpe will be <=0. Single most likely kill: it is beta, not alpha — fails "beats B&H OOS" before the holdout even runs.
novel_feasible.test_plan:
DATA (all Alpaca-SIP-fetchable, already in repo via scripts/download_data.py): 10.5yr SIP daily OHLCV. Two test surfaces. (A) Single-asset directional, the strategy as literally specified: SPY, plus 20-30 liquid large-caps + sector ETFs (XLK, XLF, XLE, ...). (B) Cross-sectional L-S variant (rank assets by predicted next-day return, long top decile / short bottom decile, dollar-neutral) on the same 80-symbol universe used by the pairs/factor work, to give it the only fair shot at being market-neutral alpha rather than beta.

CONSTRUCTION: Features = price-only, no-lookahead, point-in-time: trailing log-returns (1,5,10,21d), realized vol, RSI, distance-to-MA, normalized by a rolling window whose stats are computed ONLY on data up to t (critical — scaler fit on full series is the classic lookahead bug). Target = next-day return sign/magnitude. Model = LSTM(50), lr=1e-3, 32 batch, early-stop on a validation fold (cap epochs, do NOT run full 100 to convergence on train). Walk-forward only: fit on a rolling train window (e.g. 756d), predict the next 63d held-out block, roll. NEVER a single static train/test split. Position: long if pred>0 (variant A) or top/bottom decile (variant B). Wrap the equity curve through the existing alpca/backtest/evaluation.py evaluate() so it gets the identical scoreboard as all 57 prior cases.

CONTROLS (the repo's standard battery, must clear ALL):
1. vs Buy-and-Hold — the primary gate. Must beat B&H on RETURN, not just Sharpe (Case 2 lesson: better-Sharpe-but-not-return = beta).
2. Beta decomposition — beta_alpha() vs B&H; require alpha>0 with beta near 0. Trend-follower will show beta>0.5, alpha~0.
3. OOS / walk-forward — already structural above; report IS vs OOS Sharpe, must not collapse.
4. Out-of-regime (2016-2026 SIP) — segment_sharpes across 4 slices incl. 2018-Q4, 2020 crash, 2022 bear; require positive in a majority and specifically survive 2022 (every overfit candidate dies there).
5. Out-of-universe / fresh-symbol holdout — THE decisive gate. Train architecture+features on symbol-set TRAIN; report on a DISJOINT symbol-set HOLDOUT. Edge must hold on names it never saw (Case 18/55/56 killer).
6. Cost — re-run net of 2 bps/leg equity. Daily next-period repredicting = high turnover; apply the Case-17 cost-wall test (real anomalies died −0.41 at 2bps from ~2x/day turnover). Variant B's daily decile churn is especially exposed.
7. DSR / Bonferroni — deflate for trials. Count every architecture/feature/hyperparam tried as a trial (n_trials large because NN search space is huge); require DSR>0.90 like Case 56's gate (zoo got 0.596).
8. Shuffle placebo — re-fit on shuffled labels; if real Sharpe ~ shuffled Sharpe, the "learning" was fitted noise (Case 19 killer; an over-parameterized LSTM is the highest-risk candidate for this).
9. Survivorship PIT — only if variant B uses any selection/screen; rebuild universe with delisted names (Case 43/46). Pure price-prediction on a fixed liquid set is not selection-based, so this is secondary for variant A.

ACCEPTANCE BAR (repo standard): "validated" = clears out-of-universe (fresh symbols) AND out-of-regime (held-out years), net of 2bps, DSR>0.90, beats B&H OOS, NOT explained by beta, NOT indistinguishable from shuffle. Predicted outcome: fails control 1 or 2 (beta) for variant A; passes IS then fails control 5 (fresh-symbol) and/or 6 (cost) for variant B.

EFFORT: tractable now but heaviest of recent candidates — needs a NN dependency (tensorflow/keras or torch, not currently in requirements.txt; the harness is deliberately numpy-free) and walk-forward refit is compute-heavy. Recommend a 1-2 day spike: build the no-lookahead WF LSTM driver, run variant A on SPY + 20 names FIRST (cheap, will almost certainly show beta and can kill it before investing in variant B).
